ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301339 covers roasted coffee bean as a category; no Kenya-specific single-origin roasted bean entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers roasted coffee generically; no Kenya single-origin medium-roast specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34725008 is the closest parent substance code for coffee; no medium-roast Kenya-specific concept exists.

serving.description: 1 tablespoon ground coffee (approx. 7g dry weight, yields ~240ml brewed cup)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving of ground coffee is approximately 7g (one tablespoon), which is used to brew one 240ml cup; the 250g retail bag yields approximately 35 servings.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, roasted, ground — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14209
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted ground coffee (NDB 14209 / FDC 171890) used as the closest approved source for Kenya medium roast ground coffee beans. Values represent dry roasted ground coffee per 100g. Kenya single-origin medium roast is not separately listed in any primary regional database; USDA SR Legacy is the highest-priority approved source for this generic coffee category. Nutrient values are for the dry ground coffee product, not the brewed beverage.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee and coffee-based beverages — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: Roasted ground coffee beans contain negligible digestible carbohydrates (sugar_g confirmed 0 per USDA SR Legacy). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently classify black coffee and coffee preparations as having no measurable glycemic response. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) per Project Veritas convention for foods with no glycemic carbohydrates. GL per 7g serving = (1 × 0) / 100 = 0.0. gi_category is low per convention.

hypertension.guidance: Kenya medium roast coffee contains caffeine, which can cause transient blood pressure elevation; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to 1–2 cups per day and monitor their response, while noting that the high potassium content (2020mg/100g dry) may offer a modest vasodilatory benefit when consumed as brewed coffee.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; chlorogenic acid content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kenya medium roast coffee contains no digestible sugars or starch and has a negligible glycemic index; epidemiological evidence suggests moderate unsweetened coffee consumption may improve insulin sensitivity, making it generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-fat dairy.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Diterpenes (cafestol and kahweol) in unfiltered preparations; antioxidant polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unfiltered brewing methods (e.g., French press, plunger) release cafestol and kahweol diterpenes from Kenya coffee beans, which can raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer filtered brewing methods, and moderate consumption of filtered coffee is associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in peer-reviewed literature.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kenya Medium Roast Coffee Beans is a single-origin specialty coffee product sourced from Kenya, sold as a 250g retail bag of whole beans or ground coffee. This product is a medium roast, meaning the beans are roasted to a level that preserves the origin's characteristic bright acidity and complex fruit notes while developing moderate body and sweetness. Kenya coffee is widely regarded as among the world's finest arabica coffees, grown at high altitude in the Central Highlands and Rift Valley regions, and is known for its distinctive grapefruit, black currant, and berry flavour profile with a wine-like acidity. The product described is sold at Starbucks Singapore and offers custom grind options. Nutritionally, dry roasted ground coffee is energy-dense per 100g (approximately 331 kcal) due to its concentrated protein, fat, and complex carbohydrate content, but a standard 7g serving brewed into 240ml of black coffee contributes negligible calories, zero sugar, and zero glycemic load. Coffee is rich in potassium, niacin, and antioxidant chlorogenic acids. It contains caffeine, which provides stimulant effects and may transiently raise blood pressure. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, unsweetened black coffee has a negligible glycemic index and may support insulin sensitivity. For cardiovascular health, filtered brewing is preferred to avoid diterpene-mediated LDL elevation. This product is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, with no allergens in the bean itself.

confidence_reason: The food is a commercially sold Kenya single-origin medium roast coffee bean product (Starbucks, Singapore). No Kenya-specific single-origin roasted coffee bean entry exists in any primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee (FDC 171890), which is the highest-priority approved source available for this generic coffee category. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is from a generic roasted ground coffee entry rather than a Kenya-specific product analysis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent dry roasted ground coffee per 100g, not brewed coffee. A standard 7g serving brewed as black coffee contributes approximately 2 kcal and negligible macronutrients. 2. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee; this is naturally zero as roasting converts residual sugars. 3. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted ground coffee; naturally zero in the soluble extract context, though the dry grounds contain some insoluble fibre not typically consumed. 4. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 5. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero; naturally zero as this is a plant-derived product. 6. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero; naturally zero as ascorbic acid is destroyed during roasting. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero; naturally zero in roasted coffee. 8. vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA SR Legacy; confirmed zero. 9. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this product contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate foods. GL per 7g serving = 0.0. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2g) are reported from USDA SR Legacy. 11. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this product as it is a Kenyan single-origin coffee sold globally; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese native-language names are conventionally used for this product category. 12. The 250g retail pack is sold at Starbucks Singapore (One Fullerton) via field survey at SGD 17.70. 13. Sentinel value -1 is not used in this record; all fields are populated with confirmed numeric values from USDA SR Legacy or set to 0.0 with justification above.
